text.title: ba-planning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ba-planning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Governance section contains vague text ("approval by leadership") instead of named roles.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No named approver means no accountability; auditors and stakeholders cannot determine who has authority, and the BA cannot obtain binding sign-off.

failure-mode.description: LLM defaults to Plan-Driven for all engagements regardless of context flags.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Models anchor on whichever sample plan they last produced; always pass the engagement model and culture flags in the system prompt and validate that the output's approach matches the decision rule output for the given inputs.

failure-mode.description: Agent invents plausible-sounding stakeholders ("Director of UX Excellence") not present in the actual org chart.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hallucinated stakeholders produce engagement plans with no real recipients; constrain to a stakeholder_seed list and flag additions as TODO(reason), not as entries.

failure-mode.description: Agent produces time estimates in the plan ("Discovery: 4 weeks").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Per repo policy, no time estimates in SDD docs; strip any time estimate from agent output and replace with complexity tier (S/M/L). Time estimates are inherently unreliable and create false expectations.

failure-mode.description: Agent conflates BA Planning with Project Planning, producing Gantt charts for BA activities.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: BA Planning covers analysis activities only; schedule integration and resource management belong to the project-manager skill. Mixing them produces a document that satisfies neither purpose.
